docs.text:
Welcome! Namaste!


I am a geophysicist, currently working as a 
postdoctoral associate in the 

Imaging and Computing Group
 and 
Earth Resources Laboratory
, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).


My research interests are in:




wave-equation based imaging and inversion


seismic interferometry and its applications in both exploration and global seismology


signal processing, blind deconvolution, source separation


parallelization and developing efficient algorithms for 
high performance computing in geophysical applications




Other Personal Pages




Google Scholar Citations
 (for citation statistics)


Research Gate
 (for full text of publications)


GitHub
 (software)


ORCID


Linkedin


Earth Resources Laboratory Homepage

postdoctoral associate in the  Imaging and Computing Group  and  Earth Resources Laboratory , Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).  My research interests are in:   wave-equation based imaging and inversion  seismic interferometry and its applications in both exploration and global seismology  signal processing, blind deconvolution, source separation  parallelization and developing efficient algorithms for

docs.text:
Focused Blind Deconvolution


Blind Source Separation/ Deblending




Deblending Random Seismic Sources via Independent Component Analysis (2017) 
[slides]


Central limit theorem


Explanation of kurtosis


Convolutional mixtures 


Frequency-domain ICA applied to seismic data








Full Waveform Inversion




Full Waveform Inversion with an Auxiliary Bump Functional (2016) 
[slides]


Simplication of seismic data for inversion


Envelope-based misfit


Overcome cycle-skipping 


Multi-objective inversion strategies






Parametrization Analysis for Full Waveform Inversion (2014) 
[slides]


Ambiguities in parameterization of multi-parameter quantitative seismic imaging 






Multi-Objective Full Waveform Inversion in the Absence of Low Frequencies (2013) 
[slides]


Maximizing correlation between observed and modelled seismic data


Cycle-skipping in FWI








Seismic Interferometry




Enhancing Core-Diffracted Arrivals by Supervirtual Interferometry (2013) 
[slides]


Theory of supervirtual interferometry applied to diffractions

docs.text:
I mainly write software in 
FORTRAN
 and 
Julia
. 
While programming in Julia, I find this 
page
 really useful. 
I have shared most 
of my contribution on 
GitHub
.
I plan to share a few other 
useful subroutines here. 


Seismic Inversion Toolbox in Julia




Documentation


A simple Jupyter 
notebook
 that demonstrates 
full waveform inversion of seismic data.




Programming Utils




A Makefile to Automatically Compile f90 Source Files with Dependencies


A 
guide
 to finalize your julia package.




Signal Processing




Construct a Minimum Phase Butter-Worth Filter in Using Log Spectra




Julia Tips




use copy! whenever possible




a
=
randn
(
1000
,
1000
)


b
=
randn
(
1000
,
1000
)


@time
 
a
[
:
]
 
=
 
b
[
:
]


@time
 
a
[
:
]
 
.=
 
b
[
:
]


@time
 
copy!
(
a
,
b
)
